emails.body:
{{first_name}}

Every holiday you haven't taken is sitting in your calendar as a meeting with someone else's problem.

Is that something you're actively trying to fix?

Doug

{{first_name}}

The pattern I see most often: the founder hires an ops manager and is back in every room within six months. The hire didn't fail. The structure did.

Does that sound like where you are?

Doug

{{first_name}}

Most founders who solve this didn't fix it by hiring better people. They fixed the structure the founder was sitting inside.

Worth a call to see if the diagnostic fits?

Doug

{{first_name}}

This is the last email from me on this. If the timing's wrong or it's not relevant, fair enough.

If it shifts, you know where to find us.

Doug

{{first_name}}

Most founders at your stage describe the same week: every decision still routes through them, holidays don't happen.

Is this something you're actively trying to sort?

Doug

{{first_name}}

Most founders I talk to at your stage have already tried the obvious fix. They promoted someone and within six months were back in every room.

Does that sound like where you are?

Doug

{{first_name}}

The founders who get their time back changed what decisions were allowed to reach them. That shift doesn't happen by itself.

Worth a call?

Doug

{{first_name}}

This is the last I'll send. If the timing's wrong, fair enough.

If it ever becomes useful, the door's open.

Doug
